title: Quiz: the cost of too many indexes
prompt_md: Indexes make reads faster. What's the main downside of adding many of them to a table?
options: They make `SELECT` queries return incorrect results
They slow down writes (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) and use extra disk space
They prevent you from adding new columns later
They have no real downside, so index every column
explanation_md: Each index is a separate sorted structure the database must keep in sync, so every write has to update the table *and* every index on it — making writes slower and consuming extra storage. Indexes never change query results (they only change speed), and they don't block schema changes. "Index everything" is wrong precisely because of the write and space cost.
